# Multi-Brand Payment Services Platform

## Overview

Build a **monorepo** containing multiple Vite web apps for payment services, deployable to brand-specific subdomains with shared code and per-brand theming. One codebase per service, many deployments with different configs.

## Tech Stack

- **Build tool:** Vite (vanilla TS or React — no Next.js, no SSR)
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS with a brand-level theme config system
- **State management:** Zustand
- **Animations:** Framer Motion
- **Auth:** Phone number OTP (shared across all apps)
- **Monorepo structure:** Shared packages for auth, theming, UI components, API clients

## Brands (5 entities)

Each brand has a unique domain, legal name, and visual identity. All styling differences must be driven by a **single brand config file** reused across every service for that brand.

| Brand | Legal Entity | Domain | Border Radius | Accent | Primary | Background Style |
|-------|-------------|--------|--------------|--------|---------|-----------------|
| SuperPE | SuperPE Marketplace Pvt Ltd | `superpe.in` | `0px` | `#266EF1` | `#FFFFFF` | Line illustration |
| Spaid | Spaid ESolutions Pvt Ltd | `spaid.in` | TBD | TBD | TBD | Complex color pattern |
| KuxDistro | KuxDistro Technology Pvt Ltd | `kuxdistro.com` | TBD | TBD | TBD | B&W photograph |
| Mewt | Mewt Account Pvt Ltd | `mewt.in` | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD |
| Slongo | Slongo Technology Pvt Ltd | `slongo.in` | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD |

## Subdomain Hosting Pattern

Every service deploys to `<service>.<brand-domain>`:

| Service | SuperPE | Spaid | KuxDistro | Mewt | Slongo |
|---------|---------|-------|-----------|------|--------|
| **Wallet** | `wallet.superpe.in` | — | — | — | `wallet.slongo.in` |
| **Education** | `education.superpe.in` | `education.spaid.in` | `education.kuxdistro.com` | `education.mewt.in` | `education.slongo.in` |
| **Rent** | `rent.superpe.in` | `rent.spaid.in` | `rent.kuxdistro.com` | `rent.mewt.in` | `rent.slongo.in` |
| **Utility** | `utility.superpe.in` | `utility.spaid.in` | `utility.kuxdistro.com` | `utility.mewt.in` | `utility.slongo.in` |
| **Seller Verification** | `seller-verification.superpe.in` | `seller-verification.spaid.in` | `seller-verification.kuxdistro.com` | `seller-verification.mewt.in` | `seller-verification.slongo.in` |

The app must detect which brand to render from the subdomain/hostname at runtime.

## Authentication (Shared Module)

All apps authenticate via phone number OTP. Build this as a shared package.

**API endpoint:**
```
POST http://localhost:8000/api/v1/otp/send
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "to": "+919867808810",
  "company": "<brand-name>",
  "channel": "SMS",
  "provider": "twilio"
}
```

**UX requirements:**
- Use a **bottom sheet** UI pattern for the OTP login (not a plain centered form)
- Sheet border-radius, accent color, and background image are brand-specific (pulled from brand config)
- Brand logo displayed on the auth screen
- Phone number input with country code selector defaulting to +91

## Service 1: Wallet (SuperPE + Slongo only)

**Reference:** Clone the end-to-end flow from [mewt-app/wallet-supercheckout](https://github.com/mewt-app/wallet-supercheckout). The live reference is [ultrafastapi.com](https://ultrafastapi.com).

**Scope:** Replicate the full wallet flow — KYC, add money, P2P transfers, beneficiary management, transaction history — restyled with brand theming via Vite (the source repo is Next.js; this must be ported to Vite).

## Service 2: Education (All 5 brands)

**Post-login flow:**
1. User sees two options: **"Pay to School/Institute"** or **"Pay to Tutor"**
2. **Institute path:** Clone the flow from [mewt-app/spaid-education](https://github.com/mewt-app/spaid-education) and adapt with brand theming
3. **Tutor path:** On tap, open a bottom sheet explaining that tutor registration is required. Then launch a CRED-style registration flow (I will provide the CRED reference screens)

**Payment gateway:** Shared PG integration (same across all brands).

## Service 3: Rent (All 5 brands)

**Identical structure to Education with these differences:**
- "School/Institute" → removed entirely (no institution flow for rent)
- "Tutor" → "Landlord"
- **Mandatory rent agreement upload:** When adding a landlord, the customer must upload a rent agreement PDF/image. An AI check validates the document against the landlord name and sender name before proceeding
- Same PG flow as other services

## Service 4: Utility (All 5 brands)

**Reference:** Clone the full end-to-end flow from [mewt-app/spaid-landing](https://github.com/mewt-app/spaid-landing) and adapt with brand theming.

## Service 5: Seller Verification (All 5 brands)

**Hosted at:** `seller-verification.<brand-domain>`

**Reference:** Clone the receiver onboarding flow from [mewt-app/receiver-onboarding](https://github.com/mewt-app/receiver-onboarding). The seller/receiver receives an encrypted link, verifies via OTP, and provides PAN/bank/UPI details. Restyle with the same brand theming system used across all other services.

## UI/UX Principles (Apply Everywhere)

- **Sheet-based UI:** Bottom sheets for forms, modals, and secondary actions — not traditional page navigations
- **Brand config drives everything:** Border radius, colors, fonts, background images, logos — all from one config per brand, shared across all services
- **No plain/ugly auth screens:** The landing/login page must look polished with the brand background image and sheet-based OTP input
- **Consistent PG flow:** Payment gateway integration is identical across all services and brands

## Repo Structure Requirements

- Monorepo with shared packages: `packages/ui`, `packages/auth`, `packages/theme`, `packages/api`
- Each service is its own app: `apps/wallet`, `apps/education`, `apps/rent`, `apps/utility`, `apps/seller-verification`
- Brand config files: `brands/superpe.ts`, `brands/spaid.ts`, etc.
- Single build command per app that accepts a brand target: e.g., `BRAND=superpe vite build`
- Each build produces a static bundle deployable to the corresponding subdomain

The current repo is the `superx` plugin workspace, not the payment platform. This will be a greenfield monorepo. The spec is extremely detailed — essentially already brainstormed. Let me check the reference repos for architectural context before producing the plan.

timeline.message:
# Multi-Brand Payment Services Platform

## Overview

Build a monorepo containing **4 customer-facing web apps** and **1 seller-facing web app**, each deployed to brand-specific subdomains. All apps share a single codebase per service but render with brand-specific theming (colors, fonts, images, border-radius, backgrounds).

---

## Tech Stack

- **Build tool:** Vite (monorepo with shared packages)
- **Hosting:** Each app deployed to its own subdomain (`{service}.{brand-domain}`)
- **Authentication:** Phone number OTP on all apps via existing API:
  ```
  POST http://localhost:8000/api/v1/otp/send
  Content-Type: application/json
  {"to": "+919867808810", "company": "Wirenow", "channel": "SMS", "provider": "twilio"}
  ```

---

## Brands (5 entities)

| Brand | Entity Name | Domain | Services |
|-------|------------|--------|----------|
| SuperPE | SuperPE Marketplace Pvt Ltd | `superpe.in` | Wallet, Education, Rent, Utility |
| Spaid | Spaid eSolutions Private Limited | `spaid.in` | Education, Rent, Utility |
| KuxDistro | KuxDistro Technology Private Limited | TBD | Education, Rent, Utility |
| Mewt | Mewt Account Pvt Ltd | TBD | Education, Rent, Utility |
| Slongo | Slongo Technology Pvt Ltd | `slongo.in` | Wallet, Education, Rent, Utility |

Subdomain pattern: `wallet.superpe.in`, `education.spaid.in`, `rent.slongo.in`, etc.

---

## Brand Theming System

Each brand has a persisted theme config applied across **all** its web apps. Theme drives: accent color, primary color, border-radius, background image, fonts, and logo.

| Brand | Accent | Primary | Border Radius | Background Style |
|-------|--------|---------|---------------|-----------------|
| SuperPE | `#266EF1` (blue) | White | `0px` (sharp corners) | Line illustration |
| Spaid | TBD | TBD | TBD | Complex colorful pattern |
| KuxDistro | TBD | TBD | TBD | Black & white photography |
| Mewt | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD |
| Slongo | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD |

### Styling Requirements
- Use a **sheet-based UI pattern** (bottom sheets, modal sheets) throughout all apps
- Play with sheet border-radius as a brand differentiator
- Brand-specific background images behind the login/landing screens
- The landing page must NOT be a bare phone-number input -- wrap it in styled sheets with brand imagery to make it polished

---

## Web Apps & Flows

### 1. Wallet (`wallet.{domain}`)
**Brands:** SuperPE, Slongo only

- **Reference repo:** https://github.com/mewt-app/wallet-supercheckout
- **Reference live site:** ultrafastapi.com
- Clone the full end-to-end flow from the reference repo and replicate for SuperPE and Slongo with brand-specific theming

---

### 2. Education (`education.{domain}`)
**Brands:** All 5

- **Post-login screen:** Two options presented as cards/sheets:
  1. **Pay to School/Institute** -- clone the flow from https://github.com/mewt-app/spaid-education
  2. **Pay to Tutor** -- on tap, opens a sheet explaining that tutor registration is required, then enters the credentialing/registration flow (I will attach the cred flow separately)

---

### 3. Rent (`rent.{domain}`)
**Brands:** All 5

- **Post-login screen:** Single option:
  - **Pay to Landlord** -- same UX pattern as the Education tutor flow (sheet opens explaining landlord registration is required, then enters registration)
  - **No institution flow** (unlike Education)
- **Rent agreement upload (mandatory):**
  - When adding a new landlord, the customer must upload a rent agreement document
  - The uploaded document is sent to an AI verification endpoint that extracts and validates the landlord name and sender/tenant name
  - Block progression until AI verification passes
- **Payment gateway:** Same PG integration across all brands (shared implementation)

---

### 4. Utility (`utility.{domain}`)
**Brands:** All 5

- **Reference repo:** https://github.com/mewt-app/spaid-landing
- Clone the full end-to-end flow from the reference repo and apply brand-specific theming

---

### 5. Seller Verification (`seller-verification.{domain}`)
**Brands:** All 5

- **Reference repo:** https://github.com/mewt-app/receiver-onboarding
- This is the flow a payment **receiver** (seller/landlord/tutor/institute) goes through for onboarding and verification
- Clone the flow from the reference repo and apply brand-specific theming consistent with the wallet app styling approach (sheets, brand colors, backgrounds)

---

## Repo Structure Requirements

- **Monorepo** with shared packages for:
  - Theme/brand configuration (consumed by all apps at build or runtime based on subdomain)
  - OTP authentication flow
  - Common UI components (sheets, inputs, buttons)
  - PG integration
- Each web app is a separate Vite project/entry that imports shared packages
- Brand resolution at runtime via subdomain detection (e.g., `education.spaid.in` resolves brand = Spaid)
- Each brand has its own **policies pages** with brand-specific names and titles (I will provide policy content separately)

---

## What I Will Provide Next

1. Targets (exact domain list for subdomain deployment)
2. Cred/tutor registration flow (for Education tutor path)
3. Policy names and content per brand
4. Remaining brand theme values (Spaid, Mewt, Slongo accent colors, border-radius, etc.)
5. AI verification endpoint details for rent agreement checking

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Monorepo scaffolded with Vite, shared packages, and 5 app entry points
- [ ] Brand theming system: a single config file per brand drives all styling; adding a new brand requires only a new config
- [ ] Subdomain-based brand detection works correctly at runtime
- [ ] OTP login flow works on all apps with sheet-based styled UI
- [ ] Wallet flow cloned and functional for SuperPE and Slongo
- [ ] Education flow with institute and tutor paths functional for all brands
- [ ] Rent flow with mandatory AI-verified rent agreement upload functional for all brands
- [ ] Utility flow cloned and functional for all brands
- [ ] Seller verification flow cloned and functional for all brands
- [ ] Each app is independently deployable to its subdomain
- [ ] All apps visually match their brand theme (verified across at least SuperPE, Spaid, KuxDistro)
Revising prompt: domain for kuxdistro is kuxdistro.com and mewt is mewt.in

### 2.4 Brand Resolution

```typescript
// packages/brand-config/src/resolve.ts
export function resolveBrand(hostname: string): BrandKey {
  const brandDomains: Record<string, BrandKey> = {
    'superpe.in': 'superpe',
    'spaid.in': 'spaid',
    'kuxdistro.com': 'kuxdistro',
    'mewt.in': 'mewt',
    'slongo.in': 'slongo',
  };
  // Extract base domain: "rent.spaid.in" → "spaid.in"
  const parts = hostname.split('.');
  const baseDomain = parts.slice(-2).join('.');
  return brandDomains[baseDomain] ?? 'superpe'; // fallback
}
```

Dev mode override via `?brand=spaid` query param for local development on `localhost`.

### 3.1 Hosting & Deployment

| Concern | Approach |
|---------|----------|
| **Hosting** | Static files on existing GKE cluster (asia-south1) behind nginx, same as receiver-onboarding |
| **Build** | Each app builds independently: `cd apps/wallet && npm run build` → `dist/` |
| **Docker** | One Dockerfile per app — multi-stage (node build → nginx serve) |
| **Subdomains** | DNS wildcard `*.superpe.in`, `*.spaid.in`, etc. → load balancer → nginx routes by `$host` |
| **SSL** | Wildcard certs per domain (Let's Encrypt or existing certs) |
| **CI/CD** | GitHub Actions: on push to `main`, build affected apps, push Docker images, deploy to GKE |

## 4. SECURITY

| Concern | Approach |
|---------|----------|
| **Auth** | Phone OTP only — no passwords, no JWT storage (session-based via httpOnly cookie or short-lived token in memory) |
| **CORS** | Backend must whitelist all brand subdomains; nginx proxy in dev |
| **CSP** | Strict CSP headers in nginx: `default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'` |
| **Rate Limiting** | OTP endpoint rate-limited server-side (already implemented) |
| **Secrets** | No secrets in client code; API keys in env vars, never committed |
| **Card Encryption** | AES-256-GCM via Web Crypto API (existing pattern from supercheckout) |
| **File Upload (Rent)** | Max 10MB, accept only PDF/images, validate MIME type client-side, server validates |
| **XSS** | React's default escaping; no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` |

## 3. INFRASTRUCTURE

### 3.1 Hosting & Deployment

| Aspect | Strategy |
|--------|----------|
| **Build** | Each app built independently via `pnpm --filter @payment/wallet build` |
| **Container** | One Docker image per app. Build-time `VITE_APP_NAME` env var. Runtime brand detection via subdomain. |
| **Hosting** | Docker containers on GKE `asia-south1` (matches existing infra from supercheckout) |
| **Routing** | Nginx serves SPA with `try_files $uri /index.html` |
| **SSL** | Wildcard certs per domain (`*.superpe.in`, `*.spaid.in`, etc.) via Let's Encrypt or Cloudflare |
| **DNS** | CNAME records: `wallet.superpe.in → payment-wallet.gke-lb.ip`, etc. |

### 3.2 Subdomain Mapping

Each app container handles ALL brands for that service. A single `education` container serves:
- `education.superpe.in`
- `education.spaid.in`
- `education.kuxdistro.com`
- `education.mewt.in`
- `education.slongo.in`

Brand detection happens in JavaScript at runtime — zero server-side routing needed.

### 3.3 CI/CD Pipeline

```
Push to main
    │
    ▼
GitHub Actions
    ├── lint + typecheck (all packages)
    ├── unit tests
    ├── build all 5 apps
    ├── build Docker images (5 images)
    └── deploy to GKE (rolling update)
```

| Env | API Base | Purpose |
|-----|----------|---------|
| `development` | `http://localhost:8000` | Local dev with API proxy |
| `staging` | `https://api-staging.spaid.in` | Pre-production testing |
| `production` | `https://api.spaid.in` | Live traffic |

### 3.5 Monitoring

- **Error tracking**: Sentry (already used in spaid RN app)
- **Analytics**: PostHog or simple event logging to existing amplitude endpoint
- **Uptime**: Simple health check on each subdomain

## 4. SECURITY

| Concern | Mitigation |
|---------|-----------|
| **Auth** | JWT tokens stored in httpOnly cookies (not localStorage). Session TTL 24h. |
| **OTP** | Rate limited server-side. 30s resend cooldown client-side. Max 3 attempts before lockout UI. |
| **CORS** | API server allows `*.superpe.in`, `*.spaid.in`, `*.kuxdistro.com`, `*.mewt.in`, `*.slongo.in` |
| **CSP** | Strict CSP headers via Nginx: `script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'` |
| **Secrets** | No secrets in client code. API keys passed via env vars at build time. Crypto keys fetched from API. |
| **XSS** | React's default escaping. No `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`. Sanitize any user-uploaded filenames. |
| **File upload** | Rent agreement upload: validate file type (PDF/image), max 10MB, send to backend for AI verification. No client-side execution of uploaded content. |
| **HTTPS** | Enforced via Nginx redirect + HSTS header |

### 2.4 Brand Resolution

```typescript
// packages/brand-config/src/resolve.ts
export function resolveBrand(hostname: string): BrandKey {
  const brandDomains: Record<string, BrandKey> = {
    'superpe.in': 'superpe',
    'spaid.in': 'spaid',
    'kuxdistro.com': 'kuxdistro',
    'mewt.in': 'mewt',
    'slongo.in': 'slongo',
  };
  // Extract base domain: "rent.spaid.in" → "spaid.in"
  const parts = hostname.split('.');
  const baseDomain = parts.slice(-2).join('.');
  return brandDomains[baseDomain] ?? 'superpe'; // fallback
}
```

Dev mode override via `?brand=spaid` query param for local development on `localhost`.

VITE_API_BASE=https://api.spaid.in/backend/v2
VITE_BRAND_OVERRIDE=superpe  # dev only

# .env.production
VITE_OTP_API_BASE=https://api.spaid.in
VITE_API_BASE=https://api.spaid.in/backend/v2
```

### 3.3 Monitoring & Error Tracking

- **Sentry** (already used in spaid app) — one project, tag by brand + app
- **PostHog** (already used) — analytics events per brand

---

## 4. SECURITY

| Concern | Approach |
|---------|----------|
| **Auth** | Phone OTP only — no passwords, no JWT storage (session-based via httpOnly cookie or short-lived token in memory) |
| **CORS** | Backend must whitelist all brand subdomains; nginx proxy in dev |
| **CSP** | Strict CSP headers in nginx: `default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'` |
| **Rate Limiting** | OTP endpoint rate-limited server-side (already implemented) |
| **Secrets** | No secrets in client code; API keys in env vars, never committed |
| **Card Encryption** | AES-256-GCM via Web Crypto API (existing pattern from supercheckout) |
| **File Upload (Rent)** | Max 10MB, accept only PDF/images, validate MIME type client-side, server validates |
| **XSS** | React's default escaping; no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` |

---

## 5. SUB-PROJECTS — Decomposition & Dependency Graph

```
Phase 0: Monorepo Scaffold
    │
    ├── Phase 1a: Brand Config Package ──┐
    ├── Phase 1b: UI Components Package ─┤
    ├── Phase 1c: Auth Package ──────────┤
    └── Phase 1d: Payment Package ───────┘
                                         │
                          ┌──────────────┤ (all Phase 2 depend on Phase 1)
                          │              │
                Phase 2a: Wallet App     │
                Phase 2b: Education App  │
                Phase 2c: Rent App       │
                Phase 2d: Utility App    │
                Phase 2e: Seller Verif.  │
                          │              │
                          └──────────────┘
                                │
                          Phase 3: Integration Testing & Polish
                                │
                          Phase 4: Docker & Deployment Config
```

---

## 6. SUB-PROJECT DETAILS

### Phase 0: Monorepo Scaffold

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Scope** | Initialize npm workspace root, tsconfig, shared Vite config factory, .gitignore, dev scripts |
| **Agent** | `superx:coder` |
| **Expected Outcome** | Running `npm install` from root installs all workspace deps; `npm run dev --workspace=apps/wallet` starts dev server |
| **Acceptance Criteria** | (1) `npm install` succeeds (2) TypeScript paths resolve across packages (3) Each app dev server starts |
| **Risks** | Workspace hoisting issues with Font Awesome Pro → mitigation: `.npmrc` with `shamefully-hoist=true` or explicit deps |

### Phase 1a: Brand Config Package (`packages/brand-config`)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Scope** | Brand type definitions, 5 brand configs (SuperPE complete, others with placeholders), subdomain resolver, CSS var injection hook (`useBrand`) |
| **Agent** | `superx:coder` |
| **Expected Outcome** | `import { useBrand } from '@payment-platform/brand-config'` → returns typed brand config; CSS vars injected on `:root` |
| **Acceptance Criteria** | (1) `resolveBrand("education.spaid.in")` returns `spaid` config (2) SuperPE has all real values (3) `?brand=X` override works in dev (4) CSS vars applied to document |
| **Risks** | `.com` vs `.in` TLD handling → mitigation: explicit domain map, not pattern matching |

### Phase 1b: UI Components Package (`packages/ui`)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Scope** | Sheet (vaul-based), Button, PhoneInput, OTPInput, Card, BrandHeader, FileUpload — all consuming CSS vars for brand styling |
| **Agent** | `superx:coder` + `superx:design` |
| **Expected Outcome** | Component library that renders identically across all apps but with brand-specific colors/radius/fonts via CSS vars |
| **Acceptance Criteria** | (1) Sheet renders with brand-specific border-radius (2) Buttons use brand accent color (3) PhoneInput validates Indian numbers (4) OTPInput handles 4-6 digit codes with auto-advance (5) All components are mobile-first |
| **Risks** | vaul sheet animations on low-end Android → mitigation: `will-change` optimization, test on real device |

### Phase 1c: Auth Package (`packages/auth`)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Scope** | LoginSheet component (branded phone input + OTP in a sheet), auth API calls, auth state management (zustand), AuthProvider wrapper |
| **Agent** | `superx:coder` |
| **Expected Outcome** | Any app wraps with `<AuthProvider>` and gets login gating; LoginSheet opens as a styled branded sheet |
| **Acceptance Criteria** | (1) OTP send triggers API call (2) OTP verify stores auth token (3) LoginSheet is NOT a bare input — it's wrapped in a branded sheet with background imagery (4) Logout clears state (5) Auth persists across page refresh (sessionStorage) |
| **Risks** | OTP API format mismatch → mitigation: adapter pattern, log raw responses |

### Phase 1d: Payment Package (`packages/payment`)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Scope** | Payment method selection sheet, Juspay gateway integration, card encryption, payment status polling |
| **Agent** | `superx:coder` |
| **Expected Outcome** | Reusable payment flow that any app can invoke: `openPayment({ amount, purpose })` |
| **Acceptance Criteria** | (1) UPI, card, netbanking options rendered (2) Card details encrypted before submission (3) Payment status tracked with polling (4) Works with Juspay SDK |
| **Risks** | Juspay SDK web integration complexity → mitigation: reference existing supercheckout implementation |

### Phase 2a: Wallet App (`apps/wallet`)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Scope** | Port supercheckout flow to monorepo structure for SuperPE and Slongo brands. Pages: Login → KYC → Wallet Home → Add Money → P2P Transfer → Transaction History |
| **Agent** | `superx:coder` (isolation: worktree) |
| **Expected Outcome** | Full wallet app that mirrors `ultrafastapi.com` functionality with brand-specific theming |
| **Acceptance Criteria** | (1) SuperPE theme: blue accent, sharp corners, line illustration background (2) Slongo theme: applies correctly (3) Add money flow works (4) P2P transfer flow works (5) Transaction history displays |
| **Risks** | Complex flow with many pages → mitigation: port page-by-page from existing supercheckout, don't rewrite |

### Phase 2b: Education App (`apps/education`)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Scope** | Post-login home with two cards (School/Institute, Tutor). Institute path: state → city → school → student details → payment (ported from spaid RN app). Tutor path: registration-required sheet → registration flow (stubbed until cred flow provided) |
| **Agent** | `superx:coder` (isolation: worktree) |
| **Expected Outcome** | Working education payment app with institute BBPS flow for all 5 brands |
| **Acceptance Criteria** | (1) Two-card home screen renders (2) Institute flow: state/city/school selection works (3) Student details entry + validation (4) Payment triggers PG (5) Tutor card opens explanatory sheet (6) All 5 brands render correctly |
| **Risks** | BBPS API data format differs from RN app → mitigation: test with real API responses early |

### Phase 2c: Rent App (`apps/rent`)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Scope** | Single-card home (Pay to Landlord). Landlord registration sheet → rent agreement upload (mandatory) → AI verification stub → payment flow |
| **Agent** | `superx:coder` (isolation: worktree) |
| **Expected Outcome** | Rent payment app with document upload and AI verification integration point |
| **Acceptance Criteria** | (1) Landlord registration flow works (2) Rent agreement upload accepts PDF/images (3) AI verification endpoint called (stubbed with mock success for now) (4) Upload blocks progression until verified (5) Payment flow works post-verification |
| **Risks** | AI verification endpoint not yet available → mitigation: build with stub, document the contract |

### Phase 2d: Utility App (`apps/utility`)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Scope** | BBPS utility bill payment flow — categories (electricity, gas, water, etc.) → biller selection → bill fetch → payment |
| **Agent** | `superx:coder` (isolation: worktree) |
| **Expected Outcome** | Full BBPS utility bill payment for all 5 brands |
| **Acceptance Criteria** | (1) Category selection screen renders (2) Biller search works (3) Bill fetch and amount display works (4) Payment triggers PG (5) Brand theming applied |
| **Risks** | Missing spaid-landing reference → mitigation: build from BBPS API docs + existing patterns in spaid RN app |

### Phase 2e: Seller Verification App (`apps/seller-verification`)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Scope** | Port receiver-onboarding from Next.js to Vite. Flow: encrypted link → OTP verification → beneficiary details → bank/PAN/UPI → onboarding completion |
| **Agent** | `superx:coder` (isolation: worktree) |
| **Expected Outcome** | Seller verification app matching existing receiver-onboarding functionality but in Vite with brand theming |
| **Acceptance Criteria** | (1) Encrypted link decryption works (2) OTP flow works (3) Beneficiary details display correctly (4) Bank/PAN/UPI entry works (5) Onboarding completion API called (6) Sheet-based UI consistent with other apps |
| **Risks** | RSA+AES crypto depends on Web Crypto API — need to port from Next.js server actions to client-side → mitigation: Web Crypto API is available in all modern browsers |

### Phase 3: Integration Testing & Polish

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Scope** | Cross-app visual verification for SuperPE, Spaid, KuxDistro. Verify brand resolution, theme switching, auth flow across all apps |
| **Agent** | `superx:test-runner` + `superx:reviewer` |
| **Expected Outcome** | All 5 apps verified across 3+ brands |
| **Acceptance Criteria** | (1) Each app loads correct brand from subdomain (2) Login flow works on every app (3) No visual regressions (4) Mobile viewport looks correct |
| **Risks** | Low — integration issues caught here |

### Phase 4: Docker & Deployment Config

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Scope** | Dockerfile per app, nginx config for subdomain routing, GitHub Actions CI/CD, GKE deployment manifests |
| **Agent** | `superx:coder` |
| **Expected Outcome** | Each app independently deployable via `docker build` + GKE deploy |
| **Acceptance Criteria** | (1) `docker build` succeeds for each app (2) nginx routes subdomains correctly (3) CI builds on push to main |
| **Risks** | GKE ingress config for 25+ subdomains → mitigation: wildcard ingress rules |

---

## 7. TESTING STRATEGY

| Level | Approach | Scope |
|-------|----------|-------|
| **Unit** | Vitest (ships with Vite) | Brand resolver, OTP API adapter, payment encryption, utility functions |
| **Component** | Vitest + React Testing Library | Sheet, Button, OTPInput, PhoneInput, LoginSheet |
| **Integration** | Vitest | Auth flow end-to-end (mock API), payment flow (mock PG) |
| **E2E** | Playwright | Login → core flow → payment for each app, against staging API |
| **Visual** | Playwright screenshots | Brand theming verification — screenshot each app with each brand, compare |
| **Manual** | QA checklist | Mobile device testing (Android Chrome, iOS Safari) |

---

## 8. DEPLOYMENT PIPELINE

```
Push to main
    │
    ▼
GitHub Actions CI
    ├── Detect changed apps (nx affected or workspace filtering)
    ├── Run lint + type-check (all packages)
    ├── Run unit + component tests
    ├── Build affected apps
    ├── Run E2E tests against built apps (Playwright)
    │
    ▼
Docker Build & Push (per affected app)
    │
    ▼
Deploy to Staging (GKE)
    ├── staging-wallet.superpe.in
    ├── staging-education.spaid.in
    │   ...
    │
    ▼
Manual approval gate
    │
    ▼
Deploy to Production (GKE)
    ├── wallet.superpe.in
    ├── education.spaid.in
    │   ...
    │
    ▼
Smoke test (health check endpoints)
```

**Rollback:** Previous Docker image tag → `kubectl rollout undo`

---

## 9. AGENT DISPATCH SUMMARY

| **4** | 1x `superx:coder` | — | Phase 3 | Low |

**Total agents:** 12-14 across 4 phases
**Max parallel agents:** 5 (Phase 2a-2e, each in isolated worktree)

---

## 10. WHAT'S NEEDED FROM USER

# Multi-Brand Payment Services Platform

## Overview

Build a **monorepo** containing multiple payment service web apps (wallet, education, rent, utility, seller verification) that deploy as **brand-specific instances** via subdomain routing. Each service shares a single codebase but renders with brand-specific theming (colors, fonts, border-radius, background images).

**Tech stack:** Vite + vanilla JS/TS (NOT Next.js), CSS custom properties for theming, single-page app per service.

---

## Brands (5 Entities)

| Brand | Entity Name | Domain | Accent Color | Border Radius | Background Style |
|-------|------------|--------|-------------|---------------|-----------------|
| SuperPE | SuperPE Marketplace Pvt Ltd | `superpe.in` | `#266EF1` (blue), white primary | `0` (sharp corners) | Line illustration |
| Spaid | Spaid ESolutions Pvt Ltd | `spaid.in` | TBD | TBD | Complex color pattern |
| Kux | KuxDistro Technology Pvt Ltd | TBD | TBD | TBD | Black & white photo |
| Mewt | Mewt Account Pvt Ltd | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD |
| Slongo | Slongo Technology Pvt Ltd | `slongo.in` | TBD | TBD | TBD |

> Brand styling must be saved as a reusable config so all web apps under the same brand inherit the same theme. TBD values will be provided later.

---

## Services & Domain Matrix

| Service | SuperPE | Spaid | Kux | Mewt | Slongo | Subdomain Pattern |
|---------|---------|-------|-----|------|--------|-------------------|
| Wallet | Yes | No | No | No | Yes | `wallet.<domain>` |
| Education | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | `education.<domain>` |
| Rent | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | `rent.<domain>` |
| Utility | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | `utility.<domain>` |
| Seller Verification | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | `seller-verification.<domain>` |

---

## Architecture Requirements

### Monorepo Structure
- **One codebase per service** -- NOT one codebase per brand
- Brand theming applied at build/runtime via config (CSS custom properties, asset paths)
- Each `<service>.<domain>` deployment is the same app bundle with a different brand config injected

### Brand Theming System
- Create a centralized `brands/` config directory with per-brand JSON/TS files containing: accent color, primary color, border-radius values, font family, logo path, background image path
- Use **sheet-based UI** (bottom sheets / modal sheets) as the primary layout pattern across all services
- Brand-specific `border-radius` on sheets is a key differentiator (e.g., `0` for SuperPE = sharp, others rounded)
- Background: full-viewport brand-specific image behind the sheet UI

### Authentication (All Services)
All web apps authenticate via phone number OTP using this API:

```bash
POST http://localhost:8000/api/v1/otp/send
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "to": "+919867808810",
  "company": "Wirenow",
  "channel": "SMS",
  "provider": "twilio"
}
```

- Landing page shows a **styled sheet** with phone number input + OTP flow (NOT a bare form)
- The `company` field in the API payload should map to the current brand

---

## Service Specifications

### 1. Wallet (SuperPE + Slongo only)

**Source:** Clone end-to-end flow from [`mewt-app/wallet-supercheckout`](https://github.com/mewt-app/wallet-supercheckout)
**Reference:** Replicate the live UX of `ultrafastapi.com` / `wallet.ultrafastapi.com`

**Flow:** Phone OTP login -> KYC -> Dashboard (add money with Juspay, P2P transfers, beneficiary management, transaction history)

**Requirements:**
- Convert from Next.js to Vite
- Apply brand-specific theming (SuperPE and Slongo will look different)
- Maintain all existing functionality from the source repo

### 2. Education (All 5 brands)

**Source (institute flow):** Clone from [`mewt-app/spaid-education`](https://github.com/mewt-app/spaid-education)

**Flow:**
1. Phone OTP login
2. User sees two options: **"Pay to School/Institute"** or **"Pay to Tutor"**
3. **Institute path:** Replicate the full flow from `spaid-education` repo
4. **Tutor path:** On tap, open a sheet informing the user that tutor registration is required, then launch a CRED-style onboarding flow (card-stack UI with progressive disclosure -- reference screenshots will be provided)

### 3. Rent (All 5 brands)

**Flow:** Identical to Education with these substitutions:
- "School/Institute" -> does NOT exist (no institution flow for rent)
- "Tutor" -> **"Landlord"**
- All copy/labels changed from education terminology to rent terminology

So the flow is: Phone OTP login -> Single option: **"Pay to Landlord"** -> Sheet informing landlord registration required -> CRED-style onboarding flow

### 4. Utility (All 5 brands)

**Source:** Clone the entire webapp end-to-end from [`mewt-app/spaid-landing`](https://github.com/mewt-app/spaid-landing)

**Requirements:**
- Convert to Vite if source uses a different bundler
- Apply brand theming system
- Preserve all existing flows and functionality

### 5. Seller Verification (All 5 brands)

**Source:** Clone from [`mewt-app/receiver-onboarding`](https://github.com/mewt-app/receiver-onboarding)
**Subdomain:** `seller-verification.<domain>`

**Flow:** Receiver gets encrypted link -> OTP verification -> PAN/bank/UPI details -> onboarding complete

**Requirements:**
- Convert to Vite if source uses a different bundler
- Apply brand-specific styling consistent with each brand's wallet/education/rent apps
- Backend: `api.spaid.in` (or brand-specific API if provided later)

---

## Policies

Each brand has its own named policies (privacy, terms, refund, etc.) with brand-specific titles. Policy content and titles will be provided separately -- the system must support per-brand policy rendering from config/CMS.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Monorepo with Vite builds for all 5 services
- [ ] Brand theming system: changing one brand config file updates all services for that brand
- [ ] `wallet.superpe.in` and `wallet.slongo.in` replicate `ultrafastapi.com` wallet flow with brand theming
- [ ] `education.<any-brand-domain>` shows institute + tutor paths with correct flows
- [ ] `rent.<any-brand-domain>` shows landlord path only
- [ ] `utility.<any-brand-domain>` replicates `spaid-landing` flow
- [ ] `seller-verification.<any-brand-domain>` replicates `receiver-onboarding` flow
- [ ] OTP auth works on all services with brand-appropriate `company` field
- [ ] Sheet-based UI with brand-specific border-radius, colors, and background images
- [ ] All services share common auth, theming, and utility code via monorepo packages

Good context established. This is a **new greenfield project** -- a multi-tenant, multi-service payment platform with 5 brands and 4+ services (rent, education, utility, wallet), all sharing code but with distinct branding per entity, deployed on subdomains.

Before I dive into approaches, I want to make sure I understand the scope correctly. Here's my first question:

**Are these web apps primarily customer-facing informational/landing pages with policy pages and payment flows (e.g., "pay your rent here", "pay your tuition here"), or are they full-featured transactional applications with dashboards, user accounts, transaction history, etc.?**

In other words -- are we building:
- **(A)** Mostly static/content sites with embedded payment forms, policy pages, and branding -- think marketing + checkout
- **(B)** Full web applications with user login, dashboards, transaction management, payment processing integrations, and admin panels
- **(C)** Something in between -- lightweight apps with a payment flow + policies, no heavy dashboard but more than just static pages

Got it. You chose **B** -- full web applications with user login, dashboards, transaction management, payment processing integrations, and admin panels.

Here's the updated plan for the multi-brand, multi-service payment platform:

---

# Plan: Multi-Brand Multi-Service Payment Platform

## Architecture Overview

A **monorepo** containing shared web application code that powers **4 services** (rent, education, utility, wallet) across **5 brands**, deployed on individual subdomains. Each deployment is a full web app with auth, dashboards, transactions, and admin capabilities -- identical codebase, different branding.

```
                    ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
                    │           MONOREPO                   │
                    │                                      │
                    │  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐  │
                    │  │ packages │  │      apps         │  │
                    │  │          │  │                    │  │
                    │  │ ui       │  │  rent-app         │  │
                    │  │ config   │  │  education-app    │  │
                    │  │ types    │  │  utility-app      │  │
                    │  │ auth     │  │  wallet-app       │  │
                    │  │ payments │  │                    │  │
                    │  │ policies │  │                    │  │
                    │  └──────────┘  └──────────────────┘  │
                    └─────────────────────────────────────┘
                                    │
                      ┌─────────────┼─────────────┐
                      │             │             │
                    build         build         build
                      │             │             │
                ┌─────▼──┐   ┌─────▼──┐   ┌─────▼──┐
                │ superpe │   │ spaid  │   │ mewt   │  ... (x5 brands)
                │ wallet  │   │ wallet │   │ wallet │
                └────────┘   └────────┘   └────────┘
                      │             │             │
              wallet.superpe.in  wallet.spaid.in  wallet.mewt.in
```

---

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Choice | Why |
|-------|--------|-----|
| **Framework** | Next.js 15 (App Router) | SSR, middleware for subdomain detection, API routes for backend |
| **Monorepo** | Turborepo + pnpm workspaces | Fast builds, shared deps, parallel task execution |
| **Language** | TypeScript | Type safety across brand configs, shared packages |
| **Styling** | Tailwind CSS + CSS custom properties | Theme switching via CSS variables per brand |
| **Auth** | NextAuth.js (Auth.js v5) | Multi-provider auth, session management, role-based access |
| **Database** | PostgreSQL + Prisma | Multi-tenant data, strong schema, relations |
| **Payments** | Razorpay / PayU (configurable per brand) | Indian payment ecosystem, UPI, cards, netbanking |
| **State** | Zustand (client) + React Query (server) | Lightweight, no boilerplate |
| **Deployment** | Vercel or Docker + Nginx | Subdomain routing, env-based brand detection |

---

## Brands & Entities

| # | Brand Key | Entity Name | Domain (assumed) |
|---|-----------|-------------|------------------|
| 1 | `superpe` | SuperPe Marketplace Pvt Ltd | `*.superpe.in` |
| 2 | `spaid` | Spaid ESolutions Private Limited | `*.spaid.in` |
| 3 | `kuxdistro` | KuxDistro Technology Private Limited | `*.kuxdistro.in` |
| 4 | `mewt` | Mewt Account Pvt Ltd | `*.mewt.in` |
| 5 | `slongo` | Slongo Technology Pvt Ltd | `*.slongo.in` |

Each brand can have **some or all** of the 4 services enabled -- controlled by a brand config file.

| **Wallet** | `wallet.<brand>` | Balance, top-up, transfers, transaction history, KYC |

| **Education** | `education.<brand>` | Institution search, fee payment, payment schedules, receipts |

## Brand Configuration System

Each brand config defines everything needed to white-label any service app:

```typescript
// packages/config/types.ts
export interface BrandConfig {
  key: string;                    // "superpe" | "spaid" | ...
  entity: {
    name: string;                 // "SuperPe Marketplace Pvt Ltd"
    registeredAddress: string;
    cin: string;                  // Corporate ID
    gstin: string;
  };
  domain: string;                 // "superpe.in"
  services: {                     // Which services this brand has
    wallet: boolean;
    rent: boolean;
    education: boolean;
    utility: boolean;
  };
  theme: {
    primaryColor: string;         // "#6C5CE7"
    accentColor: string;
    backgroundColor: string;
    fontFamily: string;           // "Inter" | "Poppins" | ...
    fontUrl?: string;             // Google Fonts URL
    borderRadius: string;         // "8px" | "12px" | ...
    logoPath: string;             // "/brands/superpe/logo.svg"
    faviconPath: string;
  };
  policies: {
    slug: string;                 // "privacy-policy"
    title: string;                // "Privacy Policy"
    contentPath: string;          // Path to markdown/content
  }[];
  payments: {
    gateway: "razorpay" | "payu";
    merchantId: string;
    // Secrets via env vars, not config
  };
  support: {
    email: string;
    phone?: string;
  };
}
```

**Subdomain resolution middleware** (runs on every request in each app):

```typescript
// apps/wallet/src/middleware.ts
import { resolveBrand } from "@payment-platform/config";

export function middleware(request: NextRequest) {
  const hostname = request.headers.get("host") || "";
  // "wallet.spaid.in" -> extract "spaid"
  const brand = resolveBrand(hostname);
  
  if (!brand) return NextResponse.redirect("/404");
  
  // Inject brand into headers for server components
  const response = NextResponse.next();
  response.headers.set("x-brand", brand.key);
  return response;
}
```

**Theme injection** (root layout reads brand config, sets CSS variables):

```typescript
// apps/wallet/src/app/layout.tsx
export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
  const brand = getBrandFromHeaders();
  
  return (
    <html style={brandToCssVars(brand.theme)}>
      <body className={brand.theme.fontFamily}>
        <BrandProvider brand={brand}>
          {children}
        </BrandProvider>
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}
```

## Brand Configuration System

Each brand config defines everything needed to white-label any service app:

```typescript
// packages/config/types.ts
export interface BrandConfig {
  key: string;                    // "superpe" | "spaid" | ...
  entity: {
    name: string;                 // "SuperPe Marketplace Pvt Ltd"
    registeredAddress: string;
    cin: string;                  // Corporate ID
    gstin: string;
  };
  domain: string;                 // "superpe.in"
  services: {                     // Which services this brand has
    wallet: boolean;
    rent: boolean;
    education: boolean;
    utility: boolean;
  };
  theme: {
    primaryColor: string;         // "#6C5CE7"
    accentColor: string;
    backgroundColor: string;
    fontFamily: string;           // "Inter" | "Poppins" | ...
    fontUrl?: string;             // Google Fonts URL
    borderRadius: string;         // "8px" | "12px" | ...
    logoPath: string;             // "/brands/superpe/logo.svg"
    faviconPath: string;
  };
  policies: {
    slug: string;                 // "privacy-policy"
    title: string;                // "Privacy Policy"
    contentPath: string;          // Path to markdown/content
  }[];
  payments: {
    gateway: "razorpay" | "payu";
    merchantId: string;
    // Secrets via env vars, not config
  };
  support: {
    email: string;
    phone?: string;
  };
}
```

**Subdomain resolution middleware** (runs on every request in each app):

```typescript
// apps/wallet/src/middleware.ts
import { resolveBrand } from "@payment-platform/config";

export function middleware(request: NextRequest) {
  const hostname = request.headers.get("host") || "";
  // "wallet.spaid.in" -> extract "spaid"
  const brand = resolveBrand(hostname);
  
  if (!brand) return NextResponse.redirect("/404");
  
  // Inject brand into headers for server components
  const response = NextResponse.next();
  response.headers.set("x-brand", brand.key);
  return response;
}
```

**Theme injection** (root layout reads brand config, sets CSS variables):

```typescript
// apps/wallet/src/app/layout.tsx
export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
  const brand = getBrandFromHeaders();
  
  return (
    <html style={brandToCssVars(brand.theme)}>
      <body className={brand.theme.fontFamily}>
        <BrandProvider brand={brand}>
          {children}
        </BrandProvider>
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}
```

| **Monorepo** | Turborepo + pnpm | Best-in-class for Next.js monorepos, caching, parallel builds |
| **Framework** | Next.js 15 (App Router) | Middleware for multi-tenancy, SSR for SEO, ISR for policies |
| **Language** | TypeScript (strict) | Type safety across packages |
| **Styling** | Tailwind CSS v4 + CSS custom properties | Brand theming via CSS variables, utility-first |
| **Fonts** | `next/font` with per-brand font loading | Optimal font loading per brand |
| **State** | React Context for brand config | No heavy state management needed |
| **Content** | MDX for policies | Rich content with component embedding |
| **Build** | Turborepo pipelines | Cached, incremental builds |

### 2.3 Multi-Tenancy Strategy: Runtime Brand Resolution

```
Request: https://wallet.spaid.in/dashboard
                    │
                    ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────┐
│  Next.js Middleware              │
│  hostname = "wallet.spaid.in"   │
│  brand = resolveBrand(hostname) │
│  → brand = "spaid"              │
│  Sets: x-brand-id header        │
│  Sets: brand cookie (fallback)  │
└──────────────────────────────────┘
                    │
                    ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────┐
│  Root Layout                     │
│  Reads brand from headers       │
│  Loads BrandConfig for "spaid"  │
│  Injects CSS variables:         │
│    --color-primary: #...        │
│    --color-accent: #...         │
│    --font-family: "..."         │
│  Wraps children in BrandProvider│
└──────────────────────────────────┘
                    │
                    ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────┐
│  Page Components                 │
│  Use brand-aware UI components  │
│  Colors/fonts resolve via CSS   │
│  Logo/images from brand assets  │
│  Legal entity name from config  │
└──────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 2.5 Hostname → Brand Resolution

```typescript
// packages/brand-config/src/resolve.ts
const HOSTNAME_MAP: Record<string, string> = {
  // Generated from all brand domain configs
  "wallet.superpe.in": "superpe",
  "rent.superpe.in": "superpe",
  "wallet.spaid.in": "spaid",
  "education.spaid.in": "spaid",
  // ... etc
};

// Fallback: extract from pattern <service>.<brand>.<tld>
function resolveBrand(hostname: string): string | null {
  if (HOSTNAME_MAP[hostname]) return HOSTNAME_MAP[hostname];
  const parts = hostname.split(".");
  if (parts.length >= 3) return parts[1]; // e.g., "spaid" from "wallet.spaid.in"
  return null;
}
```

## 3. INFRASTRUCTURE

### 3.1 Hosting

| Option | Recommendation | Rationale |
|--------|---------------|-----------|
| **Vercel** | **Primary choice** | Native Turborepo + Next.js support, automatic preview deploys, edge middleware for brand resolution, easy subdomain routing |
| Cloudflare Pages | Alternative | Cheaper at scale, but less native Next.js support |
| AWS Amplify | Alternative | If AWS ecosystem required |

**Vercel Multi-App Setup:**
- Each app (wallet, rent, education, utility) = separate Vercel project
- Each Vercel project has multiple custom domains pointing to it
- Example: `wallet.superpe.in`, `wallet.spaid.in`, `wallet.kuxdistro.in` → all point to the `wallet` Vercel project

### 3.2 DNS Configuration

```
For each brand (e.g., spaid.in):
  wallet.spaid.in   → CNAME → cname.vercel-dns.com (wallet project)
  rent.spaid.in     → CNAME → cname.vercel-dns.com (rent project)
  education.spaid.in → CNAME → cname.vercel-dns.com (education project)
  utility.spaid.in  → CNAME → cname.vercel-dns.com (utility project)

Repeat for: superpe.in, kuxdistro.in, mewt.in, slongo.in
```

### 3.3 CI/CD Pipeline

```
GitHub Actions:
  push to main → Turborepo detects affected apps → deploy only changed apps
  push to PR branch → preview deployments per affected app
```

|---------|------|
| Error tracking | Sentry (free tier covers initial needs) |
| Analytics | Plausible or PostHog (privacy-friendly) |
| Uptime | Better Uptime or Vercel's built-in |

## 4. SECURITY

| Concern | Strategy |
|---------|----------|
| **HTTPS/SSL** | Automatic via Vercel for all custom domains |
| **CSP** | Content Security Policy headers in `next.config.ts` — restrict to own domains |
| **CORS** | Not applicable for static sites; if APIs added later, whitelist brand domains only |
| **Rate Limiting** | Vercel Edge Middleware rate limiting for any form submissions |
| **Secrets** | Environment variables in Vercel, never committed. `.env.example` for shape only |
| **Input Validation** | Zod schemas for any form inputs (payment flows) |
| **XSS** | React's built-in escaping + CSP headers. No `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` except MDX renderer |
| **Dependency Security** | `pnpm audit` in CI, Dependabot alerts enabled |
| **Brand Spoofing** | Middleware validates hostname against known brand domains; unknown hosts → 404 |

**Key test patterns:**
```typescript
// Test brand resolution for all hostnames
test.each(ALL_BRAND_HOSTNAMES)("resolves %s correctly", (hostname) => {
  const brand = resolveBrand(hostname);
  expect(brand).toBeDefined();
  expect(brand.theme.colors.primary).toMatch(/^#[0-9a-f]{6}$/i);
});

// Test component rendering per brand
test.each(ALL_BRANDS)("Header renders correctly for %s", (brandId) => {
  const config = getBrandConfig(brandId);
  render(<BrandProvider config={config}><Header /></BrandProvider>);
  expect(screen.getByAltText(`${config.displayName} logo`)).toBeInTheDocument();
});
```

### 2.5 Runtime Brand Detection Flow

```
Request hits Next.js middleware
        │
        ▼
Extract hostname from request headers
  e.g., "wallet.spaid.in"
        │
        ▼
Parse: service = "wallet", domain = "spaid.in"
        │
        ▼
Look up BrandConfig by domain match
        │
        ▼
Verify service is in brand's allowed services[]
        │
        ├── Service not allowed → 404 page
        │
        ▼
Inject brand ID into request headers / cookies
        │
        ▼
App layout reads brand ID → loads theme
        │
        ▼
CSS custom properties set on <html>
        │
        ▼
All components render with brand theming
```

**Middleware implementation (shared across all apps):**

```typescript
// Each app's middleware.ts
import { resolveBrand } from '@payplatform/brand-config';

export function middleware(request: NextRequest) {
  const hostname = request.headers.get('host') || '';
  const brand = resolveBrand(hostname);

  if (!brand) {
    return NextResponse.rewrite(new URL('/404', request.url));
  }

  // Inject brand ID for server components
  const response = NextResponse.next();
  response.headers.set('x-brand-id', brand.id);
  request.nextUrl.searchParams.set('brand', brand.id);
  return response;
}
```

### 2.6 Theming Strategy

CSS custom properties set at the root level, consumed by Tailwind:

```css
/* Injected dynamically based on brand */
:root {
  --color-primary: 220 90% 56%;
  --color-accent: 142 76% 36%;
  --font-heading: 'Inter', sans-serif;
  --font-body: 'DM Sans', sans-serif;
  --radius: 0.5rem;
}
```

```typescript
// tailwind.config.ts (shared)
export default {
  theme: {
    extend: {
      colors: {
        primary: 'hsl(var(--color-primary))',
        accent: 'hsl(var(--color-accent))',
      },
      fontFamily: {
        heading: 'var(--font-heading)',
        body: 'var(--font-body)',
      },

}
```

Components use `bg-primary`, `text-accent`, `font-heading` — brand colors are injected at runtime without rebuilding.

## 3. INFRASTRUCTURE

### 3.1 Hosting Strategy

| Component | Recommendation | Alternative |
|-----------|---------------|-------------|
| **App Hosting** | Vercel (native Next.js, wildcard domains, edge middleware) | Cloudflare Pages or AWS Amplify |
| **CDN** | Vercel Edge Network (included) | Cloudflare CDN |
| **DNS** | Cloudflare DNS (wildcard records) | Route 53 |
| **Asset Storage** | Git LFS for brand assets or Cloudflare R2 | S3 |

### 3.2 Domain & DNS Setup

Each brand needs a **wildcard DNS record**:

```
*.superpe.in    → CNAME → cname.vercel-dns.com
*.spaid.in      → CNAME → cname.vercel-dns.com
*.kuxdistro.in  → CNAME → cname.vercel-dns.com
*.mewt.in       → CNAME → cname.vercel-dns.com
*.slongo.in     → CNAME → cname.vercel-dns.com
```

Vercel project configuration maps all domains to the correct app:
- `wallet.*.in` → wallet app deployment
- `rent.*.in` → rent app deployment
- etc.

**Alternative (single-deployment):** Deploy one Next.js app that handles ALL services and ALL brands. Routing is done entirely via middleware. This reduces deployment complexity from 4 apps to 1 but increases blast radius.

**Recommendation:** 4 separate Vercel projects (one per service) for isolation. Each project has all 5 brand domains added.

| **CORS** | Not applicable (no cross-origin API calls from these apps to our own backends assumed) |

## Summary

This is a **white-label multi-tenant frontend platform** with:
- **1 monorepo** containing 4 Next.js apps + 4 shared packages
- **Runtime brand detection** via hostname — no per-brand builds needed
- **CSS custom properties** for zero-rebuild theming
- **MDX-based policies** per brand
- **5 phases** of implementation with up to 3 parallel agents
- **~11 sub-projects** from scaffold to visual QA

The architecture is intentionally thin on the app side — each service app is a minimal shell that imports shared UI/theming/policies. Adding a new brand is a single config file. Adding a new service is cloning an app template. Removing a brand-service combo is deleting one entry from an array.

## Key Architecture Decisions

### Brand Detection (Runtime)
```
Request → Next.js Middleware → Extract hostname → Resolve brand key
→ Inject brand config into React context → CSS variables applied → Themed render
```
- `middleware.ts` in each app reads `request.headers.get('host')`
- Maps `wallet.spaid.in` → `{ service: 'wallet', brand: 'spaid' }`
- Fallback brand configurable via `NEXT_PUBLIC_BRAND` env var (for local dev)

### Theming via CSS Variables
- Each brand config exports: `primary`, `secondary`, `accent`, `background`, `foreground`, `fontFamily`, `logoUrl`, `faviconUrl`
- `BrandThemeProvider` sets these as CSS custom properties on `<html>`
- Tailwind uses `var(--brand-primary)` etc. — zero runtime JS for styling
- Brand switch is instant, no rebuild needed

### Policy System
- Policies stored as MDX files per brand in `packages/policies/content/<brand>/`
- Each brand's policy config defines: slug, display title, effective date
- Dynamic route `app/policies/[slug]/page.tsx` resolves content from the active brand
- Supports brand-specific policy names (e.g., "Spaid Privacy Notice" vs "SuperPe Privacy Policy")

### Deployment Model
- Each app builds independently via Turborepo: `turbo run build --filter=wallet`
- Deploy to Vercel/Cloudflare with wildcard subdomain: `wallet.*`
- Brand resolved at runtime from hostname — **one build serves all brands for a given service**
- Alternatively, build per-brand with `NEXT_PUBLIC_BRAND=spaid` for static export if needed
